directives.default-src: The fallback for other fetch directives that aren't explicitly specified.
directives.script-src: Controls which scripts can be executed on the page.
directives.style-src: Controls which stylesheets can be applied to the page.
directives.img-src: Controls which images can be loaded on the page.
directives.font-src: Controls which fonts can be loaded on the page.
directives.connect-src: Controls which origins can be reached via Fetch, XHR, WebSockets, etc.
directives.child-src: Defines the valid sources for web workers and nested browsing contexts (like iframes).
directives.frame-src: Controls which origins can be embedded as iframes.
directives.object-src: Controls which origins can be embedded via <object>, <embed>, or <applet>.
directives.media-src: Controls which origins can provide audio and video resources.
directives.manifest-src: Controls which origins can provide the web app manifest, which defines how the application looks and behaves when installed on a device (e.g. name, icons, and theme).
directives.worker-src: Controls which origins can provide Web, Shared, or Service Worker scripts. Workers can complicate content security policy enforcement.
directives.base-uri: Restricts the URLs which can be used in a document's <base> element.
directives.form-action: Restricts the URLs which can be used as the target of a form submission.
directives.frame-ancestors: Restricts which origins can embed the current page as a frame.
